audit_items.proof_trace: In damon_sysfs_add_target(), t is allocated via damon_new_target() (kmalloc) at line 2180. When damon_target_has_pid(ctx) is true and find_get_pid() returns NULL (lines 2185-2188), the code jumps to destroy_targets_out before damon_add_target(ctx, t) at line 2190 — so t is never inserted into ctx->adaptive_targets. The cleanup call damon_sysfs_destroy_targets(ctx) at line 2197 only iterates targets already in the context list, so t is never freed.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A local user with sysfs write access writes a nonexistent PID to a VADDR monitoring context target (e.g., /sys/kernel/mm/damon/admin/kdamonds/0/contexts/0/targets/0/pid_target), causing find_get_pid() to return NULL and leaking sizeof(struct damon_target) bytes of kernel memory per write; repeated triggering can exhaust kernel memory.
